data.title: Plaid Technologies appoints Shawn Babcock as COO
Berkshire Hathaway may unload 27.5% Kraft Heinz stake - filing
data.description:
Berkshire Hathaway (BRK.A) (BRK.B) may unload its 27.5% stake in Kraft Heinz (KHC), according to a regulatory filing on Tuesday, potentially ending a more than decade-long investment that chairman Warren Buffett helped create back in 2015.

Kraft Heinz (KHC) disclosed in a prospectus supplement that its largest shareholder “may offer to sell, from time to time, 325,442,152 shares.” KHC shares fell nearly 4% to $22.85 following the filing.

Kraft Heinz (KHC) did not immediately respond to Seeking Alpha's request for comment.

Buffett and the Brazilian investment firm 3G Capital orchestrated the merger of Kraft and Heinz back in 2015, betting that the company’s well-known brands would deliver long-term strength.

Over time, the combination proved weaker than expected, with Berkshire Hathaway taking a $3.76B write-down on its Kraft Heinz stake last summer. Buffett said last fall that he was disappointed in Kraft Heinz' plan to split the company in two.

In September, Kraft Heinz (KHC) said that its board has unanimously approved a plan to separate the company into two independent, publicly traded companies through a tax-free spin-off. The split aims to simplify operations and let each new company focus on its own goals, improving performance while keeping the scale needed to stay competitive.

The food and beverage giant began reviewing strategic options in May.

data.title: Germany's TKMS seeks investment package to woo Canada over $12 billion submarine tender - Reuters
data.description:
• Warship builder TKMS (TKMSF) is in talks with Norwegian and German companies to offer a multi-billion-dollar investment package to Canada in a fiercely competitive submarine tender, its CEO told Reuters.
  • The talks go beyond submarines and cover possible investment commitments in rare earths, mining, artificial intelligence, and battery production for the automotive sector, Oliver Burkhard said.
  • The previously unreported investment package plan could boost TKMS (TKMSF) as it vies to win the tender after being short-listed last year along with South Korea's Hanwha Ocean to supply up to 12 state-of-the-art submarines to Canada's navy, the report added.
  • Burkhard said TKMS was in discussion with German space startup Isar Aerospace about the investment initiative but did not name the other companies involved in the talks.

data.title: European markets stay in red over Trump's Greenland rhetoric
data.description:
London (UKX) +0.03% to 10,130. UK's inflation rises to 3.4% in December. The pound was little changed at $1.344, holding above a four-week low.

Germany (DAX:IND) -0.36% to 24,613. 

France (CAC:IND) -0.02% to 8,061.

The pan-European Stoxx 600 (STOXX) traded 0.22% lower around €601.5, extending losses to a fourth straight session over U.S. President Donald Trump’s escalating rhetoric over Greenland and ongoing turmoil in global bond markets. 

The Air Force One plane carrying U.S. President Donald Trump to the World Economic Forum in Switzerland was forced to turn back late Tuesday due to what the White House called a “minor electrical issue” shortly after takeoff from Joint Base Andrews.

In the bond market, the U.S. 10-year Treasury yield was down 3 basis points to 4.27%. 

Germany's 10-year yield was down 2 basis points to 2.84%.

UK's 10-year yield was down 3 basis points to 4.43%.

Currencies: (EUR:USD) (GBP:USD) (CHF:USD)

ETFs: (NYSEARCA:EWG), (NYSE:GF), (NYSEARCA:EWI), (NYSEARCA:EWQ), (NASDAQ:FGM), (NASDAQ:DAX), (NYSEARCA:FLGR), (NYSEARCA:FXB), (NYSEARCA:EWU), (NASDAQ:FKU), (BATS:EWUS), (NYSEARCA:FLGB), (NYSEARCA:GREK)

data.title: Lynas Rare Earths in talks with U.S. DoD on rare earth price floors, quarterly revenue jumps
data.description:
Australia's Lynas Rare Earths (LYSCF) is negotiating with the U.S. Department of Defense to obtain government-backed price floors for its rare earth supplies, similar to arrangements provided to a major U.S. producer last year.

CEO Amanda Lacaze told reporters that talks with the Pentagon were delayed by last quarter’s U.S. government shutdown and that securing price support remains a central priority in the ongoing negotiations.

"…even with a passing analysis of the rare earths market, will recognize that the feature which was far the most significant for the deal that the U.S. did with MP (MP Materials) was the price protection agreement," Lacaze said on the post-earnings conference call.

Lacaze also noted that while several agreements with governments are still pending, the policies already enacted by the U.S. government have helped create more efficient and functional market dynamics.

Lynas (LYSCF) reported a 43% increase in second-quarter revenue on Wednesday, as higher selling prices more than offset a production shortfall caused by power disruptions at its Western Australia ore processing plant.

The average selling price for Lynas' product range was A$85.60 ($57.69) per kg during the quarter, higher than A$49.20 per kg a year ago.

"Market conditions are gradually improving. The relaxation of the China magnet export controls during the prior quarter translated to increased demand for NdPr from China magnet makers, accompanied by an increase in the market price from October through to December," the company said in a statement.

data.title: OpenAI unveils Stargate community plan to keep energy costs in check
Trump signs order to restrict Wall Street investors from buying single-family homes
data.description:
President Donald Trump has signed an executive order to restrict large institutional investors from buying single-family homes in an effort to help boost housing affordability.

"My Administration will take decisive action to stop Wall Street from treating America’s neighborhoods like a trading floor and empower American families to own their homes," Trump stated in the order.

"To preserve the supply of single-family homes for American families and increase the paths to homeownership, it is the policy of my Administration that large institutional investors should not buy single-family homes that could otherwise be purchased by families," he added.

Trump directed his administration to prevent federal agencies from facilitating single-family home sales to large institutional investors and promote sales to individual buyers instead. 

The order also prevents the agencies from unloading federally owned homes to large institutional investors.

The Department of Justice and the Federal Trade Commission must review substantial acquisitions by Wall Street investors of single-family homes for anti-competitive effects and prioritize enforcement of antitrust laws against coordinated vacancy and pricing strategies by these investors.

data.title: Disco GAAP EPS of ¥854.42, revenue of ¥303.83B

data.title: Metalite announces $465K private placement and $352K debt settlement
UK's inflation rises to 3.4% in December
data.description:
• The inflation rate in the UK increased to 3.4% in December from 3.2% in November, above estimates of 3.3%.
  • The CPI in the UK increased 0.4% M/M in December 2025, rebounding from a 0.2% fall in November and in line with expectations.
